title: Pittsburgh's Most Unexpectedly Viral Moments
subtitle: The posts that blew up against each account's own baseline. The accident beats the campaign.
angle: the moments that ran far past what each business's own account typically draws. Some they posted themselves, some showed up in someone else's video. Not the biggest feeds, the biggest surprises: ranked by the multiple over each business's own baseline, never by raw reach.
intro: The ranking you're about to read is not a popularity contest. Every entry is measured against the business's own typical post, which means a neighborhood dessert shop beating its baseline by a factor of 1,047 outranks a bigger account's routine performance, full stop. That's what happened to Treats n Beans in West Oakland on March 21, when a caption about Dubai chocolate and pistachios turned into 2.8 million plays against a median of roughly 197 engagements per post. And some of these businesses didn't even have to post anything: Fry Bar in Central Northside landed 181 times its own baseline inside someone else's NFL Draft restaurant guide, filmed and uploaded by a creator who just happened to include them.

rank_label: ranked by lift over a typical post
method_note: Every multiple on this list compares one moment's combined engagement, likes plus comments plus a thirtieth of its plays, against that account's own median post. Reach never ranks here; surprise does.
